title: 第 30 節
en: 5 The real Brahman, unmodified by form and name.
J. The Brahman which has been thus described (as immortal with three feet in heaven, and as Gayatri) is the same as the ether which is around us ;

8. And the ether which is around us, is the same as the ether which is within us. And the ether which is within us, 9. That is the ether within the heart. That ether in the heart (as Brahman) is omnipresent and unchanging. He who knows this obtains omnipresent and unchangeable happiness.
Thirteenth KHAivnA1.

1. For that heart there are five gates belonging to the Devas (the senses). The eastern gate is the Pra^a (up-breathing), that is the eye, that is Aditya (the sun). Let a man meditate on that as brightness (glory of countenance) and health. He who knows this, becomes bright and healthy.
2. The southern gate is the Vyana (backbreathing), that is the ear, that is the moon. Let a man meditate on that as happiness and fame. He who knows this, becomes happy and famous.
3. The western gate is the Apana (downbreathing), that is speech, that is Agni (fire). Let a man meditate on that as glory of countenance and health. He who knows this, becomes glorious and healthy.
4. The northern gate is the Samana (onbreathing), that is mind, that is Par^anya (rain). Let a man meditate on that as celebrity and beauty.

1 The meditation on the five gates and the five gate-keepers of the heart is meant to be subservient to the meditation on Brahman, as the ether in the heart, which, as it is said at the end, is actually seen and heard by the senses as being within the heart.
Ill PRAPAITtfAKA, 13 KHANDA, 8. 47 He who knows this, becomes celebrated and beautiful.
5. The upper gate is the Udana (out-breathing), that is air, that is ether. Let a man meditate on that as strength and greatness. He who knows this, becomes strong and great.

6. These are the five men of Brahman, the doorkeepers of the Svarga (heaven) world. He who knows these five men of Brahman, the door-keepers of the Svarga world, in his family a strong son is born. He who thus knows these five men of Brahman, as the door-keepers of the Svarga world, enters himself the Svarga world.

7. Now that light which shines above this heaven, higher than all, higher than everything, in the highest world, beyond which there are no other worlds, that is the same light which is within man. And of this we have this visible proof1 :

8. Namely, when we thus perceive by touch the warmth here in the body2. And of it we have this audible proof: Namely, when we thus, after stopping our ears, listen to what is like the rolling of a carriage, or the bellowing of an ox, or the sound of a burning fire3 (within the ears). Let a man meditate on this as the (Brahman) which is seen and heard.

1 The presence of Brahman in the heart of man is not to rest on the testimony of revelation only, but is here to be established by the evidence of the senses. Childish as the argument may seem to us, it shows at all events how intently the old Brahmans thought on the problem of the evidence of the invisible.
2 That warmth must come from something, just as smoke comes from fire, and this something is supposed to be Brahman in the heart.
3 Cf. Ait. Ar. Ill, 2, 4, 11-13.
He who knows this, becomes conspicuous and celebrated, yea, he becomes celebrated.
Fourteenth Khaada.
1. All this is Brahman (n.) Let a man meditate on that (visible world) as beginning, ending, and breathing 2 in it (the Brahman).
Now man is a creature of will. According to what his will is in this world, so will he be when he has departed this life. Let him therefore have this will and belief:

2. The intelligent, whose body is spirit, whose form is light, whose thoughts are true, whose nature is like ether (omnipresent and invisible), from whom all works, all desires, all sweet odours and tastes proceed ; he who embraces all this, who never speaks, and is never surprised, 3. He is my self within the heart, smaller than a corn of rice, smaller than a corn of barley, smaller than a mustard seed, smaller than a canary seed or the kernel of a canary seed. He also is my self within the heart, greater than the earth, greater than the sky, greater than heaven, greater than all these worlds.

4. He from whom all works, all desires, all sweet odours and tastes proceed, who embraces all this, who never speaks and who is never surprised, he, my self within the heart, is that Brahman (n.) When I shall have departed from hence, I shall obtain him (that Self). He who has this faith 2 has no doubt ; thus said SandWya. 3, yea, thus he said.
1 (jralan is explained by ga., born, la, absorbed, and an, breathing. It is an artificial term, but fully recognised by the Vedanta school, and always explained in this manner.
2 Or he who has faith and no doubt, will obtain this.
3 This chapter is frequently quoted as the SaWilya-vidya, Vedantasara, init; Vedanta-sutra III, 3, 31.
Ill PRAPA777AKA, 1 5 KHAiVDA, 5. 49 Fifteenth Kbanda1.

1. The chest which has the sky for its circumference and the earth for its bottom, does not decay, for the quarters are its sides, and heaven its lid above. That chest is a treasury, and all things are within it.

2. Its eastern quarter is called Guhu, its southern Sahamana, its western Ra^fli, its northern Subhuta2. The child of those quarters is Vayu, the air, and he who knows that the air is indeed the child of the quarters, never weeps for his sons. ' I know the wind to be the child of the quarters, may I never weep for my sons.'

3. 'I turn to the imperishable chest with such and such and such V 'I turn to the Pra;za (life) with such and such and such.' ' I turn to Bhu/z with such and such and such.' ' I turn to Bhuva/z with such and such and such.' { I turn to Sva/z with such and such and such.'
zh: 真實的梵天（Brahman），未被形式與名稱所變異。
J. 如此被描述的梵天（作為天國中不朽的三足者，以及蓋亞特里），與圍繞我們四周的以太是同一的；
8. 我們周圍的乙太與我們內部的乙太是同一個。我們內部的乙太， 9. 就是心中的乙太。心中的那個乙太（作為梵天）是遍在而不變的。認知此者得獲遍在而不變的幸福。
第十三講 吠陀 1
對於那顆心，有五扇屬於提婆的門（感官）。東邊的門是普拉納（pra^a，上升之呼吸），那就是眼睛，那就是阿迪提耶（Aditya，太陽）。願人沈思於此，視其為光輝（容貌的榮耀）與健康。知曉此者，將變得光輝燦爛且身體康健。
2. 南門是韻那（逆呼吸）（Vyana），即耳朵，即月亮。讓一個人以此冥思為幸福與名聲。知悉此者，將變得幸福而著名。
3. 西門是阿帕那（下呼吸），即言語，即阿格尼（火）。讓人冥想彼者為面容之榮光與健康。知此者變得榮光煥發且身體康健。
4. 北門是薩曼那（吸氣，onbreathing），即心識，即帕若尼亞（雨，Paranya）。讓人冥想於此，以之為名望與美貌。
對心的五扇門和五位守門人的冥想，目的是為了輔助對梵天（Brahman）的冥想，梵天乃心中的以太，如末尾所述，是通過感官實際看見與聽聞存在於心中的。
《往世書》(Ill Prapaitaka)，第十三章，第8節47：知曉此者，將成名聞四海，光彩照人。
5. 上方之門乃是烏達那（出息），即氣，即乙太。人應當冥想此為力量與偉大。知曉此者，便成強大而偉大。
6. 這是梵天的五位男神，斯瓦迦（Svarga）天界的守門人。凡知曉梵天的這五位男神、斯瓦迦天界的守門人者，其家中將生出強壯的兒子。凡如此知曉梵天的這五位男神、視為斯瓦迦天界的守門人者，其人自身將進入斯瓦迦天界。
7. 那閃耀於此天之上的光，超越一切，超越萬物，在最高的世界中，其上別無他界——那光與人內在的光是同一的。而我們對此有可見的證明¹：
8. 即當我們以此方式透過觸覺感知身體中此處的溫暖時²。而我們對此有此聽覺上的證明：即當我們以此方式，在塞住雙耳後，聆聽類似馬車滾動、或牛隻吼叫、或燃燒火焰之聲的東西³（在雙耳內）。讓一個人冥想此物為（婆羅門 Brahman），此物乃被看見且被聽聞者。
梵天（Brahman）存在於人心之中，不應僅仰賴啟示的見證，而是要在此以感官的證據加以確立。儘管這個論證在我們看來可能顯得幼稚，但它無論如何都足以說明古老的婆羅門人多麼專注於思考無形者之證據的問題。
那種溫暖必定來自某物，正如煙來自火，而這個某物被認為就是心中的梵天（Brahman）。
參考《愛德亞蘭尼亞奧義書》第三卷第二篇第四部分第十一至十三章。
凡知此者，必將顯赫而聞名於世，是則聞名於世矣。
第十四課
一切皆為梵天（Brahman）。願人沈思於彼（可見世界）作為始、終，並呼吸於其中（梵天）。
現在人是一個具有意志的受造物。根據他在這個世界上的意志如何，當他離開此生後也將如此。因此，讓他具有這樣的意志和信念：
2. 彼聰慧者，其身為靈，其形為光，其思為真，其性如乙太（遍在而隱沒）；自彼而出一切作為、一切欲望、一切芬芳與滋味；彼包含此一切者，從不言語，從不驚訝，3. 彼乃我心中之自我，小於一粒米，小於一粒大麥，小於一粒芥子，小於一粒金絲雀籽或金絲雀籽之仁。彼亦乃我心中之自我，大於大地，大於天空，大於天堂，大於此一切世界。
4. 一切作為、一切願欲、一切芬芳與滋味皆由他而生；他擁抱這一切，他從不言語，他從不驚訝；他，我心中的自我，就是那梵天（n.）。當我離開此處之時，我將獲得他（那自我）。具有此信心者無疑惑；撒盧迦如是說。³ 是的，他如是說。
吠陀梵語中，jralan 乃由 ga.（誕生）、la（被吸收）及 an（呼吸）解釋而得。此雖為人為造詞，然已為吠檀多學派充分認可，並始終按此方式詮釋。
或者說，具有信心而無疑惑者將得到此境。
3 本章常被引用為《薩毘利亞明論》(SaWilya-vidya)、《吠檀多綱要》(Vedantasara) 等處；《吠檀多經》(Vedanta-sutra) III, 3, 31。
梨俱吠陀第三卷，第五祈禱文，第49節　第十五讚歌。
以天空為周界、以大地為底部的箱子不會腐敗，因為四方為其側面，天界為其上蓋。那個箱子是一個寶庫，萬物都在其中。
2. 東方稱為古胡（Guhu），南方稱為薩哈曼那（Sahamana），西方稱為剌飛（Ra^fli），北方稱為蘇布陀（Subhuta）。這些方位的孩子是風神瓦尤（Vayu），即空氣，誰知道空氣確實是方位的孩子，他就絕不會為他的兒子們哭泣。「我知道風是方位的孩子，願我永不為我的兒子們哭泣。」
我轉向不朽的寶藏，以如此如此如此之物。我轉向普拉那（生命）(Prana)，以如此如此如此之物。我轉向布（Bhu），以如此如此如此之物。我轉向布瓦（Bhuva），以如此如此如此之物。我轉向蘇瓦（Sva），以如此如此如此之物。
